title: Bytes, Object Representation, Alignment, Padding, and Endianness · C Contract Brief: Alignment Requirements
summary: A device protocol works on one architecture but leaks padding and faults on a stricter-alignment target. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem alignment requirements increment in portable binary representation codec. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute and satisfy fundamental, extended, member, and allocated alignment requirements before access.
Inspect object representations through character types while separating value representation, padding, and trap risks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Alignment Requirements · Theory Alignment Requirements
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 5476. Produce bounded C evidence to encode values without copying native padding; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Alignment Requirements contract for the supplied data.
